verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein (large lumenal/extracellular domain, short cytosolic tail) that predominantly cycles between ER, ERGIC, and Golgi as a cargo receptor. However, its lumenal domain actively drives partial plasma membrane localization by overriding ER retention signals, creating a documented minority PM pool. At the PM the lumenal domain faces the extracellular space. A peptide from its extracellular domain is pharmacologically active against TGF-β signalling. This dual-localization architecture qualifies as contextual: the protein reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM anchor, but the PM is not its dominant compartment.
